purpose: Retrieval-only golden eval — measures whether the right source files / route rules / entity ids appear in the top-k for a given query, independent of any LLM answer. Run via `python financials/qa/rag_eval/run_retrieval_goldens.py`.
scoring_notes: Each golden has 0..N expected_source_files, 0..N expected_route_rules, 0..N expected_entity_ids. A golden passes when every expected item from each non-empty list appears in the corresponding retrieval result. expected_source_files are matched against the union of source_files across the top-k orchestrator hits. expected_route_rules are matched against the rule_names returned by build_routed_evidence. expected_entity_ids are matched against EntityRetriever hits in the top-k (only run when entity index is available; missing index ⇒ test is reported as 'skipped:no-index').

goldens.query: Give me a project brief for Parkway Fields
goldens.notes: Project brief tool: must surface the parkway_fields project chunk and the entity.

goldens.query: What changed for AultF B-suffix in v2.1?
goldens.notes: The 1,499-row / $4.0M B-suffix correction story. Routed rule fires before version_change.

goldens.query: What is the status of AultF SR-suffix 0139SR and 0140SR?
goldens.notes: 401 inferred-unknown rows for AultF SR-suffix lots.

goldens.query: What is HarmCo and how do commercial parcels differ from lots?
goldens.notes: HarmCo X-X commercial parcels are non-residential; not in LotState. 205 rows / $2.63M.

goldens.query: Is SctLot the same as Scarlet Ridge?
goldens.notes: v2.1 split: SctLot canonical_project = 'Scattered Lots' (not Scarlet Ridge). Zero lot-number overlap with ScaRdg.

goldens.query: Why does Harmony cost rollup need the three-tuple join?
goldens.notes: Harmony lot numbers share across MF1 vs B1 phases — must join (project, phase, lot).

goldens.query: Can I trust per-lot cost on range/shell rows?
goldens.notes: Range/shell rows = phase-grain. Total BCPD exposure = $45.75M / 4,020 rows / 8 VF codes.

goldens.query: What does it mean when a project has no matched GL cost?
goldens.notes: Missing GL match = unknown, never $0. Core data-integrity rule.

goldens.query: Can you give me an org-wide cost rollup across all entities?
goldens.notes: Hillcrest + Flagship Belmont GL coverage ends 2017-02. v2 is BCPD-scoped.

goldens.query: Why does the system use VF cost basis instead of the GL trial balance?
goldens.notes: Vertical Financials is the cost source. The decoder report describes route inference per VF code.

goldens.query: Is BCPD ready for a lot-level margin report this week?
goldens.notes: Margin-readiness tool driver: range/shell + inferred decoder + coverage caveats.

goldens.query: Where might our reports give false precision on BCPD costs?
What changed from v2.0 to v2.1 and what is the dollar impact?
Prepare me for a 30-minute review meeting with the source owners.
Draft a concise owner-level update on BCPD operating state.
What is the difference between PWFT1 and PWFS2 in Parkway Fields?
goldens.notes: PWFT1 = townhomes phase 1 (C1/C2); PWFS2 = SFR phase 2 range route.

goldens.query: How do LomHS1 and LomHT1 map to Lomond Heights phases?
goldens.notes: Both LomHS1 (SFR 101-171) and LomHT1 (TH 172-215) route to phase 2A. Pure alias-recall test — no routing rule exists today, so this exercises the entity layer.
